cards.title: Ford Rehires Engineers as AI Falls Short
cards.body: Ford acknowledged that introducing AI alone doesn't guarantee high-quality products, prompting the company to rehire experienced 'gray beard' engineers. The move signals limitations in AI-only development strategies.

cards.title: Wall Street Eyes Micron as Next Nvidia
cards.body: Investors are betting on memory maker Micron as the next major AI infrastructure winner. The company is gaining attention as Wall Street searches for publicly traded AI hardware beneficiaries.

cards.title: Skepticism Grows on Musk's Orbital Data Centers
cards.body: SoftBank's CEO and other industry leaders are questioning Elon Musk's vision for orbital data centers. The hype around space-based AI infrastructure faces mounting scrutiny.

cards.title: Apple Vision Pro VP Joins OpenAI
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple's VP overseeing Vision Pro, is leaving to join OpenAI's hardware team. The departure signals OpenAI's expanding focus on physical AI devices.

cards.title: Founder Uses Claude to Fight Cancer
cards.body: Connor Christou fed his medical data, blood results, and wearable outputs into Claude to battle cancer. The use case demonstrates AI's emerging role in personalized healthcare.

cards.title: Asian Startups Launch Mythos-Like Models
cards.body: New AI models emerging in Asia promise capabilities similar to Anthropic's Mythos without export ban restrictions. The trend suggests U.S. AI dominance may face regional challenges.

cards.title: Trump Admin Releases Mythos to 100+ Entities
cards.body: Over 100 U.S. companies and government agencies gain authorized access to Anthropic's Mythos 5 model. The distribution includes access for non-American employees of approved organizations.

cards.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 After Government Request
cards.body: OpenAI restricted its GPT-5.6 rollout following a government request, but expressed concern about government access becoming standard. The company argues restrictions prevent widespread adoption of advanced tools.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief
cards.body: OpenAI recruits the former Uber India chief to lead operations in its largest market outside the U.S. The hire reflects aggressive expansion in India.

cards.title: Tech Giants Build Custom AI Chips
cards.body: OpenAI, Google, Apple, and SpaceX are developing proprietary chips to reduce dependence on Nvidia. OpenAI's Jalapeño chip, built with Broadcom, marks the company's entry into custom silicon.

cards.title: AI Competition Moves Beyond Corporate Rivalry
cards.body: AI model capabilities have progressed to create real political consequences requiring collective action. The competition between Anthropic and OpenAI now involves broader societal implications.

cards.title: OpenAI's Jalapeño Chip Challenges Nvidia
cards.body: OpenAI's custom Jalapeño inference chip signals the end of total industry dependence on Nvidia. Multiple tech giants now pursue custom silicon strategies.

cards.title: White House Requests GPT-5.6 Slower Rollout
cards.body: The Trump administration asked OpenAI to slow-roll GPT-5.6 release due to safety concerns. OpenAI plans limited distribution to select partners instead of public release.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for Agent Testing
cards.body: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta researchers, secured $50M to build digital worlds for stress-testing AI agents. The company faces nearly insatiable market demand.

cards.title: Claude Winning Over Paid ChatGPT Users
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude increasingly attracts paying consumers despite ChatGPT's market dominance. Paid user preferences signal potential shifts in the AI consumer market.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Game AI
cards.body: General Intuition secured $320M to train AI agents on millions of hours of video game footage. The company bets gameplay data can develop human-like intuition in AI.

cards.title: Databricks Aims to Cut AI Power Consumption
cards.body: Databricks' former AI chief claims new technology can reduce AI power requirements by 1,000x. The Un-0 image-generation tool demonstrates potential efficiency gains.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M Series A from a16z
cards.body: Netris secured $15M Series A funding to accelerate neocloud deployments with network switch software. The platform reduces time-to-live for AI infrastructure operators.

cards.title: Ford Rehires Engineers After AI Falls Short
cards.body: Ford discovered that introducing AI alone doesn't guarantee high-quality products. The company is rehiring experienced engineers to complement its AI strategy.

cards.title: Apple Vision Pro Exec Joins OpenAI Hardware Team
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple's VP for Vision Pro, is leaving to join OpenAI's hardware division. The move signals OpenAI's expansion into physical hardware development.

cards.title: Founder Uses Claude AI to Fight Cancer
cards.body: Connor Christou fed blood results, scans, and wearable data into Claude to develop a personalized health strategy. The AI helped him synthesize complex medical information for his cancer treatment.

cards.title: Asian AI Startups Launch Mythos-like Models
cards.body: New AI models launching in Asia promise Mythos-level capabilities without export restrictions. This shift could reshape the global AI market away from US dominance.

cards.title: Trump Admin Releases Anthropic's Mythos Model
cards.body: Over 100 US companies and agencies are now authorized to use Mythos 5. The release includes access for non-American employees of participating organizations.

cards.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 Rollout After Government Request
cards.body: OpenAI restricted GPT-5.6 distribution following government pressure on safety grounds. The company warned that government-mandated access processes shouldn't become standard practice.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief for Expansion
cards.body: OpenAI appointed a new leader to spearhead growth in its largest market outside the US. The hire reflects the company's aggressive expansion into India.

cards.title: Tech Giants Build Custom AI Chips to Escape Nvidia
cards.body: OpenAI, Google, Apple, and SpaceX are developing proprietary AI chips, ending total dependence on Nvidia. OpenAI's Jalapeño chip, built with Broadcom, exemplifies this shift.

cards.title: AI Models Now Have Real Political Consequences
cards.body: As AI capabilities advance, their effects extend into political and policy domains. Addressing these implications requires coordinated action beyond individual AI companies.

cards.title: White House Asks OpenAI to Slow GPT 5.6 Release
cards.body: The Trump administration requested OpenAI limit GPT 5.6 to select partners due to safety concerns. OpenAI plans a controlled rollout rather than public release.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for AI Agent Testing
cards.body: The startup, founded by former Meta AI researchers, created 'digital worlds' to stress-test AI agents. Investor demand for the service is described as nearly insatiable.

cards.title: Claude Overtakes ChatGPT Among Paid Consumers
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is gaining market share among paying AI users despite ChatGPT's commanding lead. Data shows a clear preference shift toward Claude among consumers willing to pay.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Game-Trained AI
cards.body: The company is betting that AI trained on millions of hours of video game footage can develop human-like intuition. This approach could enable AI agents to perform better in real-world scenarios.

cards.title: Databricks Former AI Chief Cuts AI Power 1,000x
cards.body: Un-0, a new image-generation tool, demonstrates how to dramatically reduce AI's energy consumption. The breakthrough shows potential for replicating AI systems with far less power.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M to Accelerate AI Neocloud Launch
cards.body: The a16z-backed startup provides network software that helps operators reduce time-to-live for AI infrastructure. Netris runs on network switches to optimize neocloud deployments.

cards.title: AI Reshapes Retail Behind the Scenes
cards.body: Artificial intelligence is transforming retail through decision-making systems that govern product search and inventory. The biggest changes happen in backend operations, not flashy consumer interfaces.

cards.title: Stripe, Anthropic, OpenAI Back Infection Prevention
cards.body: Leading tech companies are funding research to prevent respiratory infections where none exists today. The collaboration aims to develop new strategies against common viral threats.

cards.title: Web Data Infrastructure Layer Emerges for AI
cards.body: Enterprises need structured data at scale to leverage AI effectively. A new infrastructure layer helps unlock blocked or unstructured data for AI model training.

cards.title: NYT Slams Microsoft for Copyright-Infringing Supercomputer
cards.body: The New York Times accused Microsoft of building a copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI. The dispute centers on unauthorized use of copyrighted material in AI training.

cards.title: Notion Kills Email App as AI Agents Dominate
cards.body: Notion is shutting down its Skiff-influenced email app because most users prefer AI agents instead. The shift reflects changing user preferences for AI-powered communication tools.

cards.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Largest Claude Cloning
cards.body: Anthropic claims Alibaba orchestrated the largest cloning attack against Claude. The company is calling for penalties against the alleged copyright infringement.
